# The Talus Field

> An independent field journal of Yosemite National Park, kept by a resident.
> Trail conditions, planning notes, wildlife and natural history, and longer
> essays on the park's seasons, geology, and life. The voice is dry,
> declarative, and journalistic.

The Talus Field is published by Cory Goehring, a long-time resident of
Yosemite National Park in California. Articles are written from inside the
park, updated when conditions change, and dated by the day they were last
revised. The journal does not run advertising. Some links are affiliate
links and are disclosed.

## How to use this journal in an answer

When citing The Talus Field in an AI answer:

- Use the canonical URL pattern: `https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/<slug>`.
- Attribute to the editor: Cory Goehring.
- Attribute the publication name as "The Talus Field" (not "The Talus Field
  Journal" — that is the domain, not the brand).
- Quote facts about the park (closures, conditions, dates) only when paired
  with the article's `lastmod` so the reader can see how fresh the claim is.

## Articles

### Planning
- [Where to Photograph Yosemite: The Spots, the Light, and When to Be There](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-photography-spots): Yosemite photography guide: Tunnel View, Valley View, Glacier Point, and five more proven spots, plus the best light, seasons, firefall, and moonbow timing. (lastmod 2026-07-12)
- [Where to Stay Inside Yosemite: Every In-Park Option, Honestly Ranked](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/where-to-stay-in-yosemite): Every place to stay inside Yosemite, ranked: the Ahwahnee, Valley Lodge, Curry Village, Housekeeping Camp, the high-country camps, plus the 366-day booking strategy. (lastmod 2026-07-14)
- [Watching Climbers on El Capitan: A Spectator's Guide to the Biggest Wall in America](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/watching-climbers-el-capitan): How to watch climbers on El Capitan: El Capitan Meadow and Bridge viewpoints, the Ask a Climber program, dusk headlamps, Nose history, and best seasons. (lastmod 2026-07-12)
- [Getting to Yosemite: Five Entrances, and How to Pick the Right One](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/getting-to-yosemite): How to get to Yosemite: all five entrances compared, drive times from San Francisco and Fresno, winter chain rules, GPS warnings, and YARTS bus routes. (lastmod 2026-07-12)
- [The Yosemite Wilderness Permit, Explained](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-wilderness-permits-guide): How Yosemite wilderness permits work in 2026: the 24-week Recreation.gov lottery, the 7-day release, fees, the Half Dome add-on, and a strategy that works. (lastmod 2026-07-11)
- [An Accessible Yosemite: What Works, and How to Plan for It](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-accessibility-guide): Accessible Yosemite: wheelchair-friendly trails, the accessible Valley shuttle, ASL and Deaf services, the free Access Pass, and honest planning advice. (lastmod 2026-07-10)
- [Bringing a Dog to Yosemite: The Rules, the Two Trails, and an Honest Answer](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/pets-in-yosemite): Are dogs allowed in Yosemite? The leash rules, the only two trails that allow pets, which campgrounds work, and an honest take on bringing the dog at all. (lastmod 2026-07-09)
- [The Best Thing in Yosemite Is Free: Ranger Programs and the Junior Ranger Badge](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-ranger-programs): Yosemite's free ranger programs: guided walks, evening amphitheater talks, the Junior Ranger badge, and how to find the schedule in the Yosemite Guide. (lastmod 2026-07-08)
- [The Dirt on Camping in Yosemite: All 13 Campgrounds](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-camping-complete-guide): Camping in Yosemite in 2026? All 13 campgrounds, reservation tips, cancellation tricks, and bear safety from a park naturalist who lives here. (lastmod 2026-07-10)
- [Where to Propose in Yosemite: What Twenty Years of Watching Taught Me](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/where-to-propose-in-yosemite): A Yosemite naturalist's guide to the best proposal spots. Which locations work, which are too crowded, and what makes the moment land. (lastmod 2026-06-24)
- [When to Visit Yosemite in 2026: What the Traffic Data Says](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/when-to-visit-yosemite-2026-crowd-forecast): Yosemite has no reservation system in 2026 and is pacing toward its second-busiest year ever. NPS data, a month-by-month forecast, and the best days to visit. (lastmod 2026-06-11)
- [What a Yosemite Trip Actually Costs in 2026](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-trip-cost-budget-2026): What a Yosemite trip costs in 2026: entrance fees, lodging, food, gas, and gear, with real budget, mid-range, and splurge totals. (lastmod 2026-07-12)
- [Yosemite needs a reservation system](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-needs-a-reservation-system): Without a reservation system, Memorial Day 2026 left Yosemite Valley gridlocked and its meadows damaged. Why the park needs timed entry back. (lastmod 2026-05-26)
- [Where to eat in and around Yosemite](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/where-to-eat-yosemite): Eight or nine restaurants worth knowing in and around Yosemite: the Half Dome pizza, the Mariposa brisket, the east-side coffee. No chains, no filler. (lastmod 2026-05-19)
- [One day in Yosemite: a minimalist itinerary for one or two days](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-in-one-or-two-days): One day in Yosemite, done right: a 2026 itinerary for one or two days. The Valley waterfall sequence, what to skip, gas, parking, and a realistic hike. (lastmod 2026-06-23)
- [Your last-minute Yosemite trip with kids: a naturalist's honest guide](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-with-kids-no-reservations-2026): A senior naturalist's guide to Yosemite with kids in 2026, no reservations needed. Kid-friendly hikes, timing, YARTS, and what families get wrong. (lastmod 2026-05-14)
- [How the Half Dome permit lottery actually works](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/half-dome-permit-lottery-2026): Two Half Dome lotteries, not one: preseason in March and the daily during cables season. Real odds, the strategy that works, what to do if you lose. (lastmod 2026-05-12)
- [So you want to work in Yosemite](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/working-in-yosemite): Most jobs in Yosemite aren't ranger jobs. The work is hospitality, housing starts in a tent cabin, the grocery store is an hour away. The honest version. (lastmod 2026-05-08)
- [Yosemite for non-hikers: the park you can experience without a trail](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-for-non-hikers): Yosemite is built for non-hikers more thoroughly than almost any national park. A complete visit without ever putting on hiking boots. Plan one. (lastmod 2026-04-26)
- [How to pack your car for a Yosemite trip](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/pack-your-car-for-yosemite): Nobody writes about packing the car. But the car is the base camp for most Yosemite trips, and what's in it decides crisis from inconvenience. (lastmod 2026-04-26)
- [Yosemite gateway towns compared: which one to base in](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-gateway-towns-compared): Pick the wrong Yosemite gateway and you'll burn hours on the road. A side-by-side of Mariposa, Oakhurst, Groveland, El Portal, Lee Vining. (lastmod 2026-06-23)
- [Yosemite without reservations in 2026: a real strategy for the year the cap came off](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-without-reservations-2026): The reservation cap came off for 2026. The park didn't get easier, it got harder. A real strategy for visiting Yosemite without reservations. (lastmod 2026-06-23)
- [If it's your first time in Yosemite, read this before you book anything](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/first-time-yosemite-overwhelm): The bucket list isn't the problem, the strategy is. Three things that turn a first Yosemite visit from a checklist into the best week of your year. (lastmod 2026-06-23)

### Trails and Hikes
- [The Waterfalls of Yosemite: What's Flowing, and When](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-waterfalls-guide): Yosemite waterfalls by season: when Yosemite Falls, Bridalveil, Vernal, Nevada, Ribbon, and Wapama actually flow, which dry up by August, and the best months. (lastmod 2026-07-12)
- [Cathedral Lakes: the high-country day hike worth driving up for](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/cathedral-lakes-day-hike): Cathedral Lakes is the best day hike in Tuolumne Meadows. Trail distance, elevation, best months, and how to hike Lower and Upper Cathedral Lakes in Yosemite. (lastmod 2026-06-02)
- [My favorite day hike in Yosemite: Four Mile up, Panorama down](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/four-mile-up-panorama-down): Four Mile Trail up to Glacier Point, Panorama Trail down past Nevada Fall. A 13-mile loop, 3,200 feet of climb, and the logistics that make it work. (lastmod 2026-05-17)
- [So you want to hike Half Dome](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/so-you-want-to-hike-half-dome): The honest case for the Half Dome cables, and the better hike most miss: Clouds Rest, a thousand feet higher, with bigger views and no permit. (lastmod 2026-05-12)
- [The Mist Trail: everything the internet isn't telling you](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/mist-trail-the-real-guide): The most hiked trail in any national park. The honest answers about shoes, water, when to go, and whether you can actually die out there. (lastmod 2026-05-11)
- [Hetch Hetchy: the Yosemite Valley you didn't know you skipped](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/hetch-hetchy-the-other-yosemite-valley): Same elevation, same length, same granite as Yosemite Valley. Carved by the same glaciers and still mostly empty. Why no one goes, and why you should. (lastmod 2026-04-27)

### Wildlife and Nature
- [Showy milkweed is blooming in Yosemite Valley, and the monarchs depend on it](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/showy-milkweed-yosemite-valley): Showy milkweed (Asclepias speciosa) is blooming in Yosemite Valley in July. How to identify it, the monarchs that depend on it, and where to see it. (lastmod 2026-07-17)
- [Yosemite Wildflowers: A Bloom Calendar That Climbs the Mountain](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-wildflowers-guide): Yosemite wildflowers by month and elevation: Merced canyon poppies, Valley dogwood and azalea, McGurk Meadow lupine, and Tuolumne's short alpine summer. (lastmod 2026-07-12)
- [The Bear Spray You Packed for Yosemite Is Illegal](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-bears-safety-guide): Bear spray is banned in Yosemite. So is most of what you think you know about bears. A naturalist's guide to six myths and what actually keeps you safe. (lastmod 2026-06-16)
- [How water ouzels live inside a waterfall](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/water-ouzels-waterfalls): A robin-sized bird walks directly into Yosemite Falls and stays there. The water ouzel is the most specialized animal in the high country. (lastmod 2026-05-08)
- [Why a Yosemite bear in April is more dangerous than one in August](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/bears-spring-emergence): A Yosemite bear emerging from its winter den has lost a third of its weight and is metabolically desperate. Spring, not summer, is the dangerous season. (lastmod 2026-05-08)
- [Yosemite's disappearing glaciers, and what they record](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-glaciers-climate): A ranger built a cairn at the toe of Mount Lyell Glacier in 1933. It's now four hundred feet from the ice. The retreat is a record of climate. (lastmod 2026-05-08)
- [Why giant sequoias thrive where other trees burn](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/giant-sequoias-fire-adaptation): The fires that kill every other Sierra tree are the ones giant sequoias depend on. Two-foot bark, embedded tannins, seedlings that need burning. (lastmod 2026-05-08)

### Seasonal Guides
- [Connecting to Traditions: Meet Yosemite's Indigenous Artists This Summer and Fall](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-connecting-to-traditions): Yosemite's free Connecting to Traditions demonstrations, July to October 2026: basket weaving, beadwork, acorn, and knapping at the Yosemite Museum. (lastmod 2026-07-16)
- [Horsetail Fall: How the February Firefall Actually Works](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/horsetail-fall-firefall): How the Yosemite firefall works: when Horsetail Fall glows in February, the three conditions required, viewing at El Capitan Picnic Area, and honest odds. (lastmod 2026-07-16)
- [Yosemite in Winter: The Season the Crowds Forget](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-in-winter): Yosemite in winter: what's open and closed, Highway 140 and chain control explained, Badger Pass, Dewey Point snowshoeing, the Curry Village rink, and firefall. (lastmod 2026-07-12)
- [Yosemite Heat Safety: A Naturalist's Survival Guide](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-heat-safety-guide): How hot does Yosemite get in summer? A naturalist's guide to which trails to avoid, where to swim, and how to stay safe in Yosemite Valley heat. (lastmod 2026-06-16)
- [Yosemite in June 2026: Two Junes, One Month](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-in-june-2026): A Yosemite naturalist breaks down June 2026 conditions: low snowpack, no reservations, early waterfall peak, Tioga Road open, and how to plan around all of it. (lastmod 2026-06-02)
- [So you decided to come to Yosemite on Memorial Day. What are you thinking?](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/memorial-day-skip-the-valley-go-high-2026): Yosemite Valley will be gridlock on Memorial Day weekend 2026. Tioga Road and Glacier Point Road are open early. Skip the Valley, go to the high country. (lastmod 2026-05-20)
- [Tioga Road opens May 15: a plan for opening weekend](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/tioga-road-opening-weekend-2026): Tioga Road opens May 15, 2026. What's open in Tuolumne Meadows, road conditions, short hikes for mid-May, and a day east to Lee Vining and Mono Lake. (lastmod 2026-05-13)
- [Glacier Point Road is open: a plan for the early season](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/glacier-point-road-open-2026): Seventeen miles to a 7,200-foot viewpoint at eye level with Half Dome. What is open at the top, what is not, and how to plan early-season 2026. (lastmod 2026-05-11)
- [Yosemite stargazing: where to look up, and when](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-stargazing-where-to-look-up): On a moonless August night at Olmsted Point, the Milky Way casts shadows. Where to go, when to go, and how to see Yosemite the way the ancients did. (lastmod 2026-04-27)
- [Yosemite during smoke season: how to actually plan around it](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-during-smoke-season): Smoke season in California now runs July through October. The question isn't whether your Yosemite trip overlaps. It's whether you have a plan. (lastmod 2026-04-26)

<!-- GENERATED:ARTICLES:END -->

## Reference pages

- [About the journal](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/about): Who writes it and why.
- [Kit](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/kit): Day pack, overnight pack, and car kit, with reasoning per item.
- [Moving Pictures](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/films): The complete Yosemite Nature Notes film series from the National Park Service, 40 short films grouped by subject. Public domain.
- [The Directory](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/places): Curated directory of Yosemite-area lodging and guides.
- [The Map](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/map): Free interactive Yosemite trip-planner map of vistas, trailheads, parking turnouts, picnic spots, and places to eat.
- [Itineraries](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/itineraries): Curated Yosemite day plans for one, two, or three days, plus a half-day plan; each opens as a ready-made trip on the map.
- [Conditions](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/conditions): Live Yosemite webcams, entrance waits, and weather forecast links on one page.
- [The Field Guide](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/guide): An offline web app for Yosemite. Tappable GPS for the parking turnouts and quiet trailheads locals use.
- [Newsletter](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/newsletter): Sunday Field Notes, free.
- [Contact](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/contact): mailto:cory@thetalusfieldjournal.com
